content: Parallel-dispatch test isolation: when smoke-testing multiple deliverables in parallel that touch shared almanac files, use the ALM_OBSERVATIONS (and analogous) env-override rather than backup-restore. Backup-restore races: D3's test runs seeded observations.yaml while D4 was mid-append, and D3's later restore clobbered D4's appended entry (self-healed here because D4 later re-wrote, but fragile). Env overrides give each test its own file; no shared state to race.
context: Observed during /furrow:work post-ship-reexamination Wave 3 where D3 and D4 were dispatched as parallel sub-agents. D4 preserved via "yq . += [...] append"; D3 restored via "cp /tmp/obs.bak observations.yaml". Both agents behaved correctly under their own contract; the race was a dispatch-level issue, not a per-agent bug. Applicable whenever an implement step dispatches parallel sub-agents that read/write the same almanac file.
